events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need interpret scenario: commercial electrical, rough-complete, observed field condition: missing box extension on deep finish. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Missing box extension on deep finish means electrical box installed too deep (recessed) for finish wall surface, missing extension ring to bring box flush with finish. NEC 314.20? Actually NEC 314.20 says boxes must be installed so that the front edge of box will be flush with finished surface or project therefrom. If plaster or other combustible finish, within 6 mm (1/4 inch) of finished surface; noncombustible within 6 mm? Actually 314.20 In Wall or Ceiling. For noncombustible surfaces, front edge can be set back not more than 6 mm (1/4 in) from finish, and for combustible surfaces must be flush or project. Also 314.16 box fill, 300.14 minimum length of conductors, 314.28 pull box sizing. Missing extension on deep finish means box is set back beyond allowed tolerance (e.g., >1/4 inch) and no extension ring installed to bring it flush. This is non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk maybe medium/high? Missing box extension can cause device mounting issues, exposed wiring, fire hazard. Commercial project, third-party inspections. Risk high? Let's assess. It is a code violation and could affect safety/inspection. But not immediate life-threatening. Use high maybe. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80? value: depth setback estimate. The prompt says estimate or interpret measurable condition. We can estimate value as 0.75 inches (19 mm) setback, exceeding 1/4 inch tolerance. Or if deep finish, maybe box is 1 inch behind finished surface. Need include value numeric reading/computed quantity. Use 0.75 (inches). Confidence 0.85. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. findings: "Box set back beyond permitted 1/4 in from finished surface; no extension ring installed." actions: "Install listed box extension ring to bring box flush with finish; verify device mounting and conductor length." rationale: "NEC 314.20 requires outlet boxes to be set flush with or within 1/4 in of finished surface; a deep-set box without an extension violates this and can damage conductors or prevent proper device installation." references: NEC 314.20, 314.16, 300.14, 314.28. Wait reference material given NEC 314.16, 300.14, 314.28. But scenario cue is box extension on deep finish, more directly 314.20. We can include 314.20 as code anchor. The instructions say apply reference material rather than quoting mechanically. We can include all. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "percent_complete": 85,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Outlet box is set back approximately 3/4 in behind the finished surface with no box extension ring installed", "Exceeds the NEC permitted setback for box front edges relative to the finished wall surface"],
  "actions": ["Install a listed box extension ring sized for the box to bring the front edge flush with the finished surface", "Verify minimum conductor length at each box and confirm device mounting is secure"],
  "rationale": "NEC 314.20 requires outlet and device boxes to be installed so the front edge is flush with or projects slightly from the finished surface, or is set back no more than 1/4 in. A box recessed about 3/4 in without an extension ring is outside tolerance, creates a device-mounting defect, and risks conductor damage.",
  "references": ["NEC 314.20", "NEC 314.16", "NEC 300.14", "NEC 314.28"]
}
